summary: Read-only verification after the fallback-reliability fix. Live SSE samples showed healthy markets now staying on mark_source='polymarket_stream' when fresh, with no immediate evidence of fresh stream marks being overwritten by REST fallback. REST fallback did not dominate the sampled frames.
verification_evidence: Read the post-fix fallback gate in dashboard_server.py around _stream_positions_mark() after the state snapshot.
Captured five live SSE positions_mark frames from /api/stream/state while 3 open positions were active.
Observed fresh live cards carrying mark_source='polymarket_stream' with current_price_stale=false across multiple frames.
Observed stale=true at frame level only when one market lacked a fresh mark; unaffected markets still retained stream-driven values and mark_source='polymarket_stream'.
Did not observe rest_fallback replacing a fresh stream mark in the sampled verification window.
residual_risks: Verification window was short; no live rest_fallback event occurred in the sampled SSE frames after the fix, so the fallback-only-when-stale rule is only partially verified from code path plus live stream behavior.

One sampled market still showed mark_source='polymarket_stream' with current_price=null/current_price_stale=true, indicating upstream stream freshness for that slug can still degrade independently of this fix.
